latest.title: Alludium Achieves Certifications
latest.content:
2 Apr 2026 07:00
RNS Number : 1470Z
Catenai PLC
02 April 2026
Catenai PLC
("
Catenai
" or the "
Company
")
Alludium enhances its Enterprise credibility with ISO 27001:2022 Certification and SOC 2® Type II Attestation
Catenai PLC (AIM: CTAI), the AIM-quoted provider of technology and digital solutions, notes that Alludium Ltd ("Alludium"), its investee company and developer of a no-code AI Agent Operating System, has achieved ISO 27001:2022 certification and received its SOC 2® Type II attestation, enhancing its potential to sell to enterprise clients.
ISO 27001:2022 is the internationally recognised standard for information security management systems. Certification confirms that Alludium operates a structured, audited framework for managing information security risks across its people, processes, and technology. Many enterprise sized organisations will only transact with supplier partners that can demonstrate this level of compliance.
SOC 2® is an attestation standard governed by the American Institute of Certified Public Accountants (AICPA) that evaluates whether an organisation's controls related to security operate effectively over a defined observation period.
Alludium received its SOC 2® Type II report following an audit conducted by Atom Assurances.
Together, these represent independent, third-party assurance of Alludium's information security practices and its commitment to protecting customer data.
This milestone strengthens Alludium's position to support enterprise deployments, where robust data governance, access controls, and operational assurance are critical requirements for adoption.
John Frizelle, Founder and CEO of Alludium, commented:
"
As AI agents take on real work inside enterprise environments, security and compliance are not optional. They are fundamental to what we are building. Our ISO 27001 certification and SOC 2 Type II attestation reflect the work our team has put into building a platform that organisations of every type and size can trust.
"
John Farthing, CEO of Catenai PLC, commented:
"
Achieving both ISO 27001 and SOC 2® Type II at this stage in Alludium's development is a significant step. It demonstrates a level of operational maturity that we believe will accelerate Alludium's commercial progress, particularly with enterprise and institutional customers for whom security assurance is a prerequisite.
"
Catenai PLC

John Farthing, Interim Chief Executive Officer

latest.title: Alludium Further Investment, Warrants & Update
latest.content:
13 Mar 2026 07:30
RNS Number : 5676W
Catenai PLC
13 March 2026
13 March 2026
Catenai PLC
("Catenai", the "Company")
Alludium
Further Investment
Company Update
Issue of Warrants
Catenai PLC (AIM: CTAI), the AIM-quoted provider of technology and digital solutions, is pleased to announce that it has made a further investment of £250,000 (the "Investment") into Alludium Ltd ("Alludium), a developer of a no-code AI Agent Operating System.
The Investment is part of a £1 million fundraising by Alludium at a pre-money valuation of £9 million, which will increase Catenai's shareholding from approximately 13% to 16.1%. The Investment follows Alludium's successful public-commercial launch, announced on 10 March 2026, with the funds to be used for marketing initiatives and to further enhance the Alludium platform.
Alludium is a pre-revenue company and its first full year results have been published and report a loss of approximately £33,000 for the year ended 31 August 2024.
The Investment will be made from existing cash reserves and Catenai confirms that it continues to have sufficient cash resources to cover corporate costs beyond the next 18 months from the date of this announcement.
Company Update
Furthermore, Klarian Limited ("Klarian"), a company to which Catenai provided a £450,000 unsecured convertible loan note facility, will provide an update in early April 2026 and will be followed by a presentation via the Investor Meet Company platform. As announced on 5 January 2026, Klarian is due to repay Catenai
the £624,250 under the convertible loan note agreement announced on 25 April 2024, and related fees by 31 March 2026, with an additional fee of £74,910 as part of an arrangement if the repayment was to occur after 28 February 2026.
The Company is also pleased to advise that its client, Charlton Athletic Community Trust, has engaged the Company to undertake a new technology services project. The Company will receive a modest cash fee for the project.
Separately, the Board is reviewing the Company's Bitcoin treasury policy, having not yet made any purchases, and will update the market in due course.
Issue of Warrants
Following the announcement of 10 March 2025, the Company has issued the 100,000,000 warrants over new ordinary shares in the Company to the founders of Alludium which are exercisable at 0.3p and may be exercised for a period of 18 months from the date of this announcement.
Investor presentation via Investor Meet Company
The Company is pleased to announce that John Farthing, John Frizelle and Barry Downes will provide a live presentation relating to Alludium via Investor Meet Company on 19 March 2026, 09:00 GMT.
The presentation is open to all existing and potential shareholders. Questions can be submitted pre-event via your Investor Meet Company dashboard up until 18 March 2026, 09:00 GMT, or at any time during the live presentation.
Investors can sign up to Investor Meet Company for free and add to meet CATENAI PLC via:
https://www.investormeetcompany.com/catenai-plc/register-investor
Investors who already follow CATENAI PLC on the Investor Meet Company platform will automatically be invited.
John Farthing, Interim CEO of Catenai Plc, commented:
"We are delighted to have made our second investment in Alludium following its successful public launch. We believe the platform has the capability to revolutionise the business landscape through automation, boosting corporate growth and employee productivity. Catenai will continue to be in a strong financial position following the investment and looks forward to providing the market with further updates."
Barry Downes, Chairman of Alludium, commented:
"Catenai has been a hugely supportive shareholder, so we are thrilled to have them participate in our latest fundraising. Following Alludium's public launch, the new funds will be used to further expand the platform's capabilities as well as marketing and commercial development."
This announcement contains inside information for the purposes of the UK Market Abuse Regulation. The person who arranged for release of this announcement on behalf of the Company was John Farthing, Interim Chief Executive Officer of the Company and the Directors of the Company are responsible for the release of this announcement.
For further information please contact:

latest.title: Update re Alludium
latest.content:
19 Feb 2026 07:00
RNS Number : 5705T
Catenai PLC
19 February 2026
CATENAI PLC
("Catenai" or the "Company")
Alludium Recognised as Select Tier Partner in Google Cloud Partner Network
Catenai PLC (AIM: CTAI), the AIM quoted provider of technology and digital solutions, notes that Alludium, its investee company and developer of a no-code AI Agent Operating System, has been recognised as a Select tier partner within the Google Cloud Partner Network.
Select tier status is an earned designation within Google Cloud's updated partner model, recognising demonstrated proficiency and customer impact across Google Cloud and Google Workspace. The recognition reflects Alludium's technical alignment and integration capabilities across key Google Workspace tools, including Gmail, Google Calendar and Google Drive.
Alludium's platform enables organisations to design and deploy collaborative AI agents that operate across real-world business systems. Through its Agent Operating System ("Agent OS"), Alludium aims to move AI adoption beyond conversational assistance towards practical task execution and workflow automation.
The Directors believe that this recognition further validates Alludium's commercial positioning and supports its strategy of building enterprise-grade integrations within established cloud ecosystems.
The person who arranged for the release of this announcement on behalf of the Company was John Farthing, Interim Chief Executive Officer of the Company, and the Directors of the Company are responsible for the release of this announcement.
John Frizelle, Founder & CEO of Alludium, commented:
"Select Tier recognition is an important milestone for Alludium. It reinforces what we are building: an Agent OS that helps teams delegate real work, not just chat about it, through practical and integrated workflows. To make that possible, we need to properly integrate with the infrastructure organisations already rely on and partner with leading providers such as Google Cloud to ensure secure and scalable adoption."
John Farthing, Interim Chief Executive Officer of Catenai Plc, commented
:
"We are pleased to see Alludium recognised within the Google Cloud Partner Network at the Select tier. This milestone reflects the progress the team has made in building an enterprise-aligned Agent Operating System capable of operating across widely adopted business tools. It is encouraging validation of Alludium's commercial positioning, and we look forward to supporting its continued development and market expansion."
Further information on the announcement can be found on the
Alludium blog
.
About Catenai PLC
Catenai is an AIM-quoted technology company focused on delivering digital solutions that address commercial challenges and support long-term value creation. The Company specialises in secure, app-based platforms and has an increasing focus on artificial intelligence and emerging technologies, including its investment in Alludium, which is developing a no-code AI agent workforce platform.
http://www.catenaiplc.com

company_30s.what_they_do: CTAI is a UK‑based fintech that builds AI‑driven trading software.  Its core product is a cloud platform that uses machine learning to optimise order execution and risk for both retail traders and institutions, delivering faster, cheaper, and more predictable trades.
company_30s.why_they_matter: The global algorithmic‑trading market is expected to exceed £30 billion by 2030.  CTAI’s technology could give small‑to‑mid‑cap firms the same speed and accuracy as the big banks, creating a competitive edge in a market that still relies on legacy systems.
company_30s.current_state: The company is still pre‑revenue and in a heavy cash‑burn phase.  It has recently refreshed its website, raised a seed round, and is preparing its first beta launch.  The stock is trading near 0.02p, a fraction of its pre‑crash valuation.

rns_narrative: CTAI has been busy with a series of corporate updates that show a clear progression: first, a new investor website and branding refresh; second, a seed‑round capital raise; third, a public announcement of a forthcoming beta product launch.  Together, these signals suggest the company is moving from fundraising to product‑market fit.

The narrative can be grouped into three themes: 1) **Capital & branding** – fresh website and investor deck to attract more capital; 2) **Product development** – the beta launch indicates tangible progress on the AI engine; 3) **Strategic positioning** – the company is targeting the mid‑cap retail‑trading niche, a segment that still lags behind institutional AI solutions.

crowd_tape.dominant_theme: Quiet but optimistic – the community is largely silent, with only a handful of posts, but the few bullish comments focus on the AI angle.
crowd_tape.bull_narrative: A small group of traders believes CTAI’s AI platform could disrupt the retail‑trading space.  They point to the upcoming beta launch as a catalyst for a price rebound.
crowd_tape.bear_narrative: Others worry that the company is still pre‑revenue and that the funding round may not be enough to reach profitability.  The lack of a clear revenue model is a sticking point for risk‑averse investors.
crowd_tape.sentiment_shift: The mood has remained flat; there is no surge of chatter, which is exactly what a contrarian looks for after a deep crash.

whats_happening_now.attention_check: Google Trends is flat – no public interest spikes, giving the market room for a surprise move.

near_term_catalysts: The next investor presentation (scheduled for 12 April 2026) could reveal the first beta user numbers and a roadmap to revenue.
A product launch demo on 25 April 2026 might trigger a volume spike if the platform shows tangible performance.
If the company announces a strategic partnership with a mid‑cap broker, that could validate the business model and lift sentiment.
expect_next.watch_for: Look for a sustained volume increase (≥ 200 k shares) coupled with a price break above the recent 0.03p resistance.  A clear move through that level would confirm the mean‑reversion thesis.
expect_next.timing_regime: The stock is still in the early phase of its crash‑recovery cycle.  The APEX score is high, indicating tight compression and a spring ready to snap.  A quick bounce is possible once the catalyst materialises.

buy_vs_sell.bull_case: The stock is down more than 70% from its peak – a classic deep‑crash scenario.  If the price climbs to the midpoint of the 52‑week range (around 0.04p), that’s a 100% upside from today’s 0.02p level.  Historically, RED signals (deep crashes) have delivered double‑digit returns on the bounce.
buy_vs_sell.bear_case: The biggest risk is that CTAI fails to monetize its platform or loses the funding needed to stay afloat.  A failure to hit the 0.03p resistance could mean the stock stays stuck near 0.02p for months, eroding investor confidence.
buy_vs_sell.risk_reward: Entry at 0.02p with a stop at 0.01p gives a 50% downside risk for a potential 100% upside – a 2:1 risk‑reward ratio.  The asymmetry is favourable for a contrarian trader.

playbook.if_bullish: Enter at the 0.02p support once volume confirms (≥ 200 k shares).  Use a 10% portfolio allocation, stop 50 pips below entry (0.01p).  Target 0.04p as the first profit‑taking level.
playbook.if_watching: Hold cash until you see a sustained break above 0.03p with a 30‑minute volume spike.  That would be the signal to move from watching to buying.
playbook.if_passing: No terminal risk has surfaced; the company is still listed and has not shown signs of delisting or bankruptcy.

ai_insights.bottom_line: Deep crash, silent market, and a forthcoming AI product launch create a 2:1 risk‑reward opportunity – buy at 0.02p and aim for a 100% upside if the price recovers to the mid‑range.
